title: aquilegia vulgaris var. brevistyla
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. brevistyla, also known as the red columbine or dwarf red columbine, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The medicinal properties of Aquilegia vulgaris var. brevistyla are said to provide health benefits such as relieving fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
